evidences.title: Building an Asynchronous OHLCV Data Downloader for ...
evidences.content: ``` async def main(): client = BinanceClient() symbols = ["BTCUSDT", "ETHUSDT", "BNBUSDT", "XRPUSDT", "XLMUSDT"] interval = "15m" start = int(time() 1000) - 5 365 24 60 60 1000 # 5 years ago end = int(time() 1000) st = time() data = await client.download_ohlcv_data(symbols, interval, start, end) et = int((time() - st) 1000) no_of_klines = sum(len(v) for v in data.values()) print(f"got {no_of_klines} klines in {et}ms") for symbol, ohlcv in data.items(): print(f"{symbol}: {len(ohlcv)} candles downloaded")if __name__ == "__main__": asyncio.run(main()) async def main "BTCUSDT" "ETHUSDT" "BNBUSDT" "XRPUSDT" "XLMUSDT" "15m" int 1000 5 365 24 60 60 1000 [...] creates concurrent tasks to fetch OHLCV data for each symbol in the provided list, over the specified time range and interval. Parameters: ----------- symbols : List[str] A list of trading pair symbols (e.g., ['BTCUSDT', 'ETHUSDT']). interval : str The candlestick interval (e.g., '1m', '1h', '1d'). start : int The start time in milliseconds since the Unix epoch. end : int The end time in milliseconds since the Unix epoch. Returns: -------- Dict[str, List[List[Any]]] A dictionary where each key is a symbol and the corresponding val...

evidences.title: Binance.US API Documentation: Introduction
evidences.content: + Previously some requests failed if the `startTime` == `endTime`. + Now, all API requests that accept `startTime` and `endTime` allow the parameters to be equal. This applies to the following requests: - `GET /api/v3/aggTrades` - `GET /api/v3/klines` - `GET /api/v3/allOrderList` - `GET /api/v3/allOrders` - `GET /api/v3/myTrades` Changes to Filter Evaluation: + Previous behavior: `LOT_SIZE` and `MARKET_LOT_SIZE` required that (`quantity` - `minQty`) % `stepSize` == 0. + New behavior changed to: (`quantity` % `stepSize`) == 0. Bug fix with reverse `MARKET` orders (i.e. `MARKET` using `quoteOrderQty`): [...] Fixed error message for querying archived orders(status `CANCELED` or `EXPIRED` where `executedQty` == 0 in the last 90 days): + Previous error message: `{` `"code": -2013,` `"msg": "Order does not exist."` `}` + Now error message: `{` `"code": -2026,` `"msg": "Order was canceled or expired with no executed qty over 90 days ago and has been archived."` `}` Behavior for API requests with `startTime` and `endTime`: [...] > Response ``` [{" a ": 874844, " p ": "0.00379700 ", " q ": "0.05000000 ", " f ": 981493, " l ": 981493, " T ": 1637128220041, " m ": true, " M ": true}] ``` `...

evidences.content: > The difference between `startTime` and `endTime` can only be up to 200 days > Between `startTime` and `endTime`, the most recent `limit` data from `endTime` will be returned: > If `startTime` and `endTime` are not sent, current timestamp will be set as `endTime`, and the most recent data will be returned. > If `startTime` is sent only, the timestamp of 200 days after `startTime` will be set as `endTime`(up to the current time) > If `endTime` is sent only, the timestamp of 200 days before `endTime` will be set as `startTime` ## Response Example​ [...] ## Response Example​ `[ [ 1591256400000, // Open time "9653.69440000", // Open "9653.69640000", // High "9651.38600000", // Low "9651.55200000", // Close (or latest price) "0 ", // Ignore 1591256459999, // Close time "0", // Ignore 60, // Number of bisic data "0", // Ignore "0", // Ignore "0" // Ignore ]]` Previous Continuous Contract Kline Candlestick DataNext Mark Price Kline Candlestick Data API Description HTTP Request Request Weight Request Parameters Response Example Copyright © 2026 Binance. evidences.title: How to get a Crypto Price change (from Binance) in an interval ...
evidences.content: The URL below shows the changes on the price of the pair `YFI/USDT` with the minimum price change that the endpoint `api/v1/klines` can provide: `&interval=1m`. `YFI/USDT` `api/v1/klines` `&interval=1m` I looked through all the Binance API for around 2 hours tonight and I couldn't find a way to get data inferior to 1 minute. I wish I could find something like this: where `&interval=1s` mean `1 second`. `&interval=1s` `1 second` Using the Binance API, is there a way to get this information? Brian Tompsett - 汤莱恩's user avatar graffiti75's user avatar ## 2 Answers 2 According to the Binance API documentation, there is no chart interval lower than `1 minute` for klines: [...] `1 minute` `Kline/Candlestick chart intervals: m -> minutes; h -> hours; d -> days; w -> weeks; M -> months 1m 3m 5m 15m 30m 1h 2h 4h 6h 8h 12h 1d 3d 1w 1M` One possibility could be to fetch old trades and then generate an `OHLC(V)` table on your own. `OHLC(V)` fbardos's user avatar ## Comments Add a comment Updated answer as of 2025-05-04. According to the Market data endpoints page, the new intervals are | Interval | interval value | --- | | seconds | 1s | | minutes | 1m, 3m, 5m, 15m, 30m | | hours | 1h, 2h,...

evidences.title: get_historical_klines() takes forever and gives no output · Issue #1416
evidences.content: Meanwhile, using `requests` works as expected: import requests url = "" params = { 'symbol': 'ETHBTC', 'interval': '1m', 'startTime': 1210685792000, 'limit': 1 } response = requests.get(url, params=params) print(response.json()) OUTPUT: I tested it in several environments and it's always the same behaviour, as described above. More over, few days ago `get_historical_klines()` worked and I used it to populate database with timestamps of earlies klines per symbol. Does someone know something? Maybe Binance changed something on its side? Reactions are currently unavailable ## Metadata ## Metadata ### Assignees ### Labels question No projects No milestone None yet No branches or pull requests ## Issue actions [...] earliest\_kline = get\_earliest\_kline(client, "ETHBTC") if earliest\_kline: print(f"Earliest kline timestamp for ETHBTC: {datetime.fromtimestamp(earliest\_kline / 1000)}") else: print("No klines found for ETHBTC since 1 Jan, 2008.") It should have returned first available kline, due to too early startTime (which was very much intentional), but it returns nothing. It does not throw any exceptions, but instantly sends requests. evidences.title: ET to UTC Converter - Time.now
evidences.content: EDT 9:00 AM → UTC 1:00 PM EDT 5:00 PM → UTC 9:00 PM 4-hour difference during summer International Coordination EST Midnight → UTC 5:00 AM EDT Midnight → UTC 4:00 AM UTC used for global scheduling Technical Applications Server logs → UTC timestamps API calls → UTC standards UTC preferred for technical systems #### Conversion Tips & Best Practices • Add 5 hours to EST (winter) or 4 hours to EDT (summer) to get UTC • UTC is the preferred standard for international business, aviation, and technical applications • UTC is also known as "Zulu Time" in military and aviation contexts • For practical purposes, UTC and GMT are the same, though UTC is the modern standard • Always use 24-hour format when expressing UTC time (no AM/PM) [...] Time Difference: UTC is 5 hours ahead of EST (winter) and 4 hours ahead of EDT (summer). The difference changes twice yearly with daylight saving transitions. #### Daylight Saving Time Impact Winter Period (EST): From first Sunday in November to second Sunday in March, ET is UTC-5, making UTC 5 hours ahead Summer Period (EDT): From second Sunday in March to first Sunday in November, ET is UTC-4, making UTC 4 hours ahead UTC Stability: UTC never changes an...

Time zones use hours and minutes offset from Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), most a whole number of hours, a few are offset by 30 or 45 minutes. evidences.content: ### What are the national holidays in the EST time zone? National holidays: New Year's Day: Celebrated on January 1st, marking the start of the new year. Independence Day: Observed on July 4th, with parades, fireworks, and celebrations throughout the region. Labor Day: Celebrated on the first Monday in September, honoring American workers and their contributions. Thanksgiving Day: Observed on the fourth Thursday in November, a major holiday for family gatherings and feasting. Christmas Day: Celebrated on December 25th, marked by family gatherings, feasts, and festive celebrations. ### What is some local trivia or geography about the EST region?
